title: Consume target-extension contract from command-generation
lane_outcome: AW records and proves that new targets declare projection rules, runtime dependencies, adapter or wrapper shape, packaging, conformance execution, support declaration, and target-runtime quirks without owning product operation semantics or per-operation feature logic.
purpose_for_parent: Clarifies the target-extension layer and keeps reusable target machinery out of AW-specific runtime code.
technical_strategy: Tighten the lane strategy before the first slice starts.
acceptance_boundary: All lane slices have landed, lane proof aggregation is satisfied, and residual work is routed.
proof_strategy: A target-support declaration can place a target into applicable generated artifacts and conformance cases once implementation exists; checks prevent targets from becoming per-operation maintenance surfaces.

evidence: AW target_support.json consumes command-generation target-extension contracts; contract tooling validates CG API consumption, implemented Python projection, planned TypeScript exclusion, and semantic-ownership rejection. Proof: doctor planning healthy; tests/test_contract_tooling.py 71 passed; contract tooling check passed; structured inventory passed after staging; generated command package and schema-reference checks passed; maintainer surfaces, lint-workspace, and test-workspace passed.
known_gaps: No residual work for #1480. Remaining #1476 work stays in the decomposition lanes for TypeScript direct operation conformance, IR-backed conformance cases, wrapper demotion/test boundary, and closure guardrails.

residual_lane_work: No residual work for #1480. Remaining #1476 work stays in the decomposition lanes for TypeScript direct operation conformance, IR-backed conformance cases, wrapper demotion/test boundary, and closure guardrails.
lane_to_epic_contribution: Clarifies the target-extension layer and proves AW consumes reusable command-generation target support without target-specific product operation semantics or per-operation feature maintenance.

closeout_state.summary: Clarifies the target-extension layer and proves AW consumes reusable command-generation target support without target-specific product operation semantics or per-operation feature maintenance.
closeout_state.residual_work: No residual work for #1480. Remaining #1476 work stays in the decomposition lanes for TypeScript direct operation conformance, IR-backed conformance cases, wrapper demotion/test boundary, and closure guardrails.

claim_authorization.renderer_rule: Renderers must choose only templates whose required claim class and closure action are authorized here; unsafe prose examples are diagnostics, not the enforcement model.

self_review.question: Does the delivered work satisfy the active human intent?
